svg.title: Demo B2: LiteLLM virtual keys — OpenAI box shifted right
svg.text: Demo B2 · LiteLLM virtual keys — the key is the identity
Three containers. App holds only a virtual key. LiteLLM maps it to the real key. Postgres persists the key vault.
Docker host · one compose.yml, three services
• never sees the real OpenAI key
① AUTH — does this key exist? revoked?
② MODEL — is this model allowed?
• only LiteLLM holds the real key
The architectural lesson: the virtual key IS the identity — auth, model, budget, rate limit, routing all keyed on it
A proxy credential minted by admin.
Looks like a real API key but is
Change the key → change all policy.
key revoked or does not exist.
Do NOT retry — will always fail.
over budget OR over rate limit.
model not allowed for this key.
Fix key policy or model name.
• Revoking a key = one DB row update.
gateway restart does not wipe keys.
• Pin the LiteLLM version in compose.
Bundle 1 · Layer B · Slot B2 — Virtual keys: LiteLLM + Postgres key vault
